text:
Powered byOutside

Dygert celebrates gold in women's team pursuit and won bronze in TT.(Photo: SEBASTIEN BOZON/AFP via Getty Images)

Team USAleaves Paris with a modern record of six medals in theOlympic Gamesand big momentum going into Los Angeles 2028.



Jennifer Valentewon gold Sunday to close out the Paris Olympics on a high note for the best Olympic run in a non-boycott Olympic Games for American cycling.

“This is the pinnacle of my career, and of anyone’s career, to win an Olympic gold medal,” Valente said. “There’s nothing better than this.”

That sentiment echoed across a rejuvenated and energizedUSA Cyclingorganization that was on its knees in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic four years ago, something that ravaged membership contributions and forced budget cutbacks.

In fact, USA Cycling went to Tokyo 2020 with one quarter of its typical Olympic staff, and returned with only three medals, its worst performance since Athens 2004 when USA won two medals.

Team USA roared into Paris intent on winning medals and raising the bar ahead of the next Olympic Games set for home roads in 2028 in Los Angeles.

With medals in every cycling discipline — in road, track, mountain bike and BMX — USA Cycling surpassed itspre-Games goal of “three to four” medals.

Valente’s final gold medal Sunday pushed the team past the modern record of five total medals earned at both Beijing 2008 and Rio de Janeiro 2016.

With three golds — in women’s team pursuit, women’s omnium and women’s road racing — Team USA betters the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Games with two gold medals, with Kristin Armstrong in time trial and Connor Fields in men’s BMX racing, as part of five total medals.

With six medals in Paris — all won by female cyclists — Team USA also sets a new record medal haul in the best Olympic Games performance in a non-boycott year.

“It’s been a really long week of racing,” Valente said Sunday. “I raced (team pursuit) qualifying over a week ago, and we had some really good performances, so to pull it off on the last day, I’m really excited.”

Jennifer Valente defends gold in Women’s Omnium at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games! 🇺🇸Read More:https://t.co/kLYzC4HPFLpic.twitter.com/GnC4fM95fx



How good was Paris?

The all-time record for Team USA in a modern Games came in 1984 Los Angeles when Americans won nine cycling medals. That was a boycott year, when the Soviet bloc did not send athletes to L.A., four years after the U.S. boycotted the 1980 Summer Games in Moscow over the Afghanistan war.

Three golds in Paris — Valente’s omnium and women’s team pursuit in track racing coupled withKristen Faulknerin the women’s road race — is the most Team USA has won in cycling since 1984, when the Americans took four gold medals.

Pre-war Olympics saw Americans win cycling medals in 1900, 1904, and 1912, and there was a U.S. medals drought all the way to the 1984 Games, when a new generation of homegrown cyclists rode the cycling boom to Olympic success.

Americans have done well in track cycling, time trialing, and BMX over the past several Olympic Games, but this year saw breakout medals in road racing and mountain biking.

Faulkner’s gold was the first American Olympic medal in road racing in 40 years, while Haley Batten won silver for America’s best-ever result in mountain biking, a discipline invented by Americans more than 50 years ago.

Paris also kept alive an important streak and helps carry momentum toward Los Angeles.

The big success in Paris means that Team USA has medaled in every Olympic Games since 1984, with only one medal with bronze in women’s sprint with Connie Paraskevin-Young in Seoul 1988.

After two medals in each Olympic Games in 1992, 2000, and 2004 — and three in Atlanta 1996 and Tokyo 2020 — the U.S. team won four medals in London 2012, and five medals in 2008 Beijing and 2016 Rio de Janeiro.

Hopes are already high for an even better performance in Los Angeles 2028, when the Americans will be racing in front of home crowds.

USA Cycling will be pushing athlete development and there should be a financial windfall with the successes in Paris as well as the hype going into a host Games.

Many of the medalists in Paris will be back for another shot at Olympic glory in Los Angeles.

American cycling also played an important role in helping the U.S. end the Paris Games atop the medal table Sunday.

The U.S. entered Sunday’s final day of competition with 38 golds, one fewer than China’s 39. The battle went back and forth across Sunday’s final day of competition that also included weight lifting, women’s basketball and marathon.

China won a gold earlier in the day, and Valente’s gold Sunday afternoon helped the U.S. to pull within one going into the women’s basketball final, where the American team was highly favored (and won).

Both the U.S. and China finished the Paris Games with 40 gold medals, but the United States was ranked first with more total medals across the Games, with 126 to 91. Six of those were from American cycling.

Here are the important milestones from Paris:
title: Team USA celebrates modern-era best with 6 Medals in Paris
author: Andrew Hood
description: American women dominate Paris Olympics with record-breaking cycling medal haul for Team USA: 'There's nothing better than this.'
